params.text:
Your role & its limits

As a care worker you spend more time with people than almost anyone else on the team, so you are often the first to notice a change. Your role is to support everyday comfort and dignity, follow the care plan, and report anything new or worrying to a nurse or senior straight away.

  • Support: help with comfort, cleanliness and gentle company.
  • Observe: notice changes in pain, mood, eating or breathing.
  • Escalate: pass concerns on promptly so the team can act.

Working as part of a team

End of life care is always delivered by a multidisciplinary team — district nurses, GPs, specialist palliative care nurses, and sometimes hospice staff, therapists and social workers. Each brings something you do not, and decisions about medicines, symptoms and treatment are made together.

Knowing the limits of your role keeps people safe. You do not diagnose, change medicines or make decisions about treatment. What you do brilliantly is notice, comfort and communicate — and raise the alarm when something needs a professional's attention.

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap.
params.textField: Your role is to support *comfort* and *dignity* within the *care plan*, to *observe* changes closely, and to *escalate* new symptoms to a nurse or senior.
